roles.description: RBAC roles scoped to this tenant. Assign permissions per role; users get the union of permissions across all roles they hold.

platformPerms.title: Platform permissions
platformPerms.description: The global permission keys available for assignment to any role.

create.title: New role
create.description: Create a custom role for this tenant. Assign permissions afterwards.

permsSheet.title: Permissions — {{name}}
permsSheet.description: Toggle to grant or revoke. Changes apply immediately to every user holding this role.

available.title: Available permissions
available.description: Permissions you can add to this role. Granting takes effect immediately.
available.empty: Every permission is already granted to this role.

permissions.description: The complete catalogue of permission keys you can grant to roles. These are global to the platform; bindings to roles live under each tenant.

policies.description: Tenant-wide security policy. Applies to every login, every session, every API call against this tenant.

network.title: Network policy
network.description: IP allowlist takes precedence: if non-empty, only matching CIDRs may sign in. Denylist always blocks.
network.allowlistHelp: Empty = allow from anywhere (subject to denylist).

password.title: Password policy
password.description: Enforced at signup, password change, and password reset.

session.title: Session policy
session.description: Affects new sessions only; existing sessions keep their original lifetime.

mfa.title: MFA enforcement
mfa.description: How aggressively users are pushed toward enrolling a second factor.
mfa.optional: Optional — users may enrol but aren't required
mfa.adminOnly: Admins only — require MFA for owner/admin roles
mfa.noteHelp: Note: this policy isn't enforced at login yet — only the separate risk-based step-up (adaptive MFA) applies today.

relationships.description: Fine-grained, relationship-based access (ReBAC). Tuples assert "object relation subject"; a check resolves direct grants and usersets (e.g. group:eng#member) recursively. Complements roles (RBAC) and policies (ABAC).

check.title: Check access
check.description: Does a user have a relation on an object? (resolves usersets)

tuples.title: Tuples on an object
tuples.description: Enter an object to list its relationship tuples.

graph.title: Identity Graph
graph.description: Expand all subjects reachable from an object+relation — a privilege-path graph showing who/what can reach a resource and through which chain of relationships.
graph.empty: No relationships found from this object+relation.

resources.description: The protected resources RBAC permissions are scoped to. Derived from the permission catalogue — each resource exposes a set of actions you can grant to roles.

catalogue.title: Resource catalogue
catalogue.description: One row per resource, with the actions it exposes.
catalogue.empty: No permissions registered, so no resources to show.

check.title: Access Tester
check.description: Evaluate a permission for a specific user and see exactly why access is granted or denied — including which role granted it and whether it came directly or through a group.
check.runDescription: Permissions use the resource:action shape, e.g. users:read.
check.userIdHelp: The UUID of the user to evaluate.
check.resultDescription: The decision and the grant-path trace the authorization engine walked.
check.resultPlaceholder: Run a check to see the allow/deny decision and how it was reached.
check.grantPathsHint: Each path is an independent reason the permission is held.
check.noGrantPath: Allowed, but no explicit grant path was returned.

groupRoles.title: Roles
groupRoles.description: Members of this group inherit these roles’ permissions.
groupRoles.emptyTitle: No roles granted to this group yet.
groupRoles.emptyDescription: Use the selector above to grant a role — every member inherits its permissions.
groupRoles.revokeTitle: Remove this role from the group?
groupRoles.revokeDescriptionNamed: Members will lose the permissions granted by {{name}} unless they hold it through another group or a direct assignment.
groupRoles.revokeDescriptionFallback: Members will lose this role’s permissions.
